root_text.iti69:2.1: “Yassa kassaci, bhikkhave, bhikkhussa vā bhikkhuniyā vā rāgo appahīno, doso appahīno, moho appahīno—
root_text.iti69:2.2: ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, ‘atiṇṇo samuddaṁ saūmiṁ savīciṁ sāvaṭṭaṁ sagahaṁ sarakkhasaṁ’.
root_text.iti69:2.3: Yassa kassaci, bhikkhave, bhikkhussa vā bhikkhuniyā vā rāgo pahīno, doso pahīno, moho pahīno—
root_text.iti69:2.4: ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, ‘atari samuddaṁ saūmiṁ savīciṁ sāvaṭṭaṁ sagahaṁ sarakkhasaṁ, tiṇṇo pāraṅgato thale tiṭṭhati brāhmaṇo’”ti.
root_text.iti69:5.1: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.

translation_text.iti69:1.1: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.
translation_text.iti69:2.1: “Whoever has not given up greed, hate, and delusion
translation_text.iti69:2.2: is said to have not crossed over the ocean with its waves and whirlpools, its saltwater crocodiles and monsters.
translation_text.iti69:2.3: Whoever has given up greed, hate, and delusion
translation_text.iti69:2.4: is said to have crossed over the ocean with its waves and whirlpools, its saltwater crocodiles and monsters. Crossed over and gone to the far shore, that brahmin stands on solid ground.”
translation_text.iti69:3.1: “Those in whom greed, hate, and ignorance
translation_text.iti69:3.3: have crossed the ocean so hard to cross,
translation_text.iti69:3.4: with its saltwater crocodiles and monsters, <j>its waves and dangers.
translation_text.iti69:4.1: They’ve slipped their chains, given up death, and have no attachments.
translation_text.iti69:4.2: They’ve given up suffering, <j>so there are no more future lives.
translation_text.iti69:4.3: They’ve disappeared and cannot be defined;
translation_text.iti69:4.4: they’ve confounded the King of Death, I say.”
translation_text.iti69:5.1: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.
